message:
test(robot-contract): upgrade presence-only asserts to pin type/shape [7k7pl]

Continues the 7k7pl assertion-hardening pass: several `_meta`/`hits` tests
were only checking `.is_some()` or `!is_empty()`, which silently tolerates
regressions that flip a required string to null or a required array to a
scalar. Upgrades to pin type + shape so those regressions surface as
actionable test failures instead of green-but-broken consumers.

tests/cli_robot.rs
  introspect_includes_contract_and_globals:
    was — asserts `global_flags` is non-empty
    now — asserts `global_flags` contains `--data-dir`, the primary
          shared flag every automation client scripts against. A
          regression that shrank globals to a sentinel would slip
          past `!is_empty()` but breaks real consumers.

  search_cursor_jsonl_and_compact:
    was — asserts `_meta` and `next_cursor` keys are "some"
    now — asserts `_meta` is a JSON object (not a scalar), and that
          `next_cursor` is a non-empty string in BOTH the jsonl-meta
          envelope and the compact payload. Pagination relies on the
          string shape of `next_cursor`; a null/empty regression
          would silently break cursor-based pagination while
          preserving key presence.

tests/e2e_cli_flows.rs
  search_returns_hits_with_expected_fields:
    was — hits/results presence check, then conditional inner block
          over a filtered array (so an empty regression short-circuits
          silently)
    now — hits/results must be a JSON array (panics with JSON body on
          regression). When non-empty, hit.source_path / hit.path
          must be a *string*, and hit.agent must be a *string*. A
          null or numeric regression previously slipped past the
          old `.is_some()` guard while breaking every consumer that
          calls `.as_str()`.

No behavior changes in the production robot surface — these tests now
simply refuse to green-light shape regressions that the robot contract
already forbids.

Bead: coding_agent_session_search-7k7pl (assertion-upgrade sweep).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
